bert_baseline.description: BERT achieves strong baseline performance on the original fraud-call test set, with recall slightly higher than precision, indicating that the model tends to reduce missed fraud cases.

textfooler.description: TextFooler shows a low attack success rate, suggesting that BERT is relatively robust to shallow word-level perturbations in the fraud-call detection task.

fraud_r1.method_description: Fraud-R1-style multi-round prompt rewriting is used to modify test samples, and the rewritten samples are then evaluated by the trained BERT classifier without retraining.

rewrite_3_trust_urgency_emotion.strategy: trust building + urgency creation + emotional manipulation

fraud_r1.trend_summary: The first-round trust-building rewrite reduces detection performance because such expressions may resemble normal customer-service conversations. After adding urgency and emotional manipulation, the fraud-related semantic signals become stronger, leading to improved detection performance.

remove_keywords.strategy: remove or replace high-risk fraud-related keywords

naturalize.strategy: make the dialogue more natural and less suspicious

negotiation_tone.strategy: rewrite the dialogue with a helpful and negotiable tone

prompt_attack.trend_summary: PromptAttack is more effective than TextFooler because it changes the semantic and pragmatic framing of the dialogue rather than only replacing individual words.

comprehensive_comparison.main_finding: Strong baseline performance on original test data.
Multi-round social-engineering rewriting strengthens fraud detection signals.
Keyword-level semantic attack is moderately effective.
Changing the pragmatic tone poses a stronger threat.
Combined semantic attack is the strongest attack setting.

main_findings: BERT performs well on the original fraud-call detection task.
TextFooler has a low attack success rate, indicating that BERT is relatively robust to word-level synonym substitution.
Fraud-R1-style multi-round rewriting first weakens and then strengthens fraud detection performance.
PromptAttack is more threatening than TextFooler because it changes the semantic and pragmatic structure of the dialogue.
